mechanism_narrative: When real retail activity trends upward, measured by positive 3-month growth in RSAFS, near-term earnings expectations improve and the S&P 500 earns positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value was 424481 for 2013-07-01 available by the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by non-positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of walk-forward observations or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals weakening household income and recession risk; when UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vintage value was 7.4 for 2013-07-01 available by the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE rises are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if downside hit rate is not above the unconditional negative-return frequency.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains at or below 0.25%, investors price future policy tightening and lower equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises > 3% while FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value was 0.09 for 2013-07-01 available by the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-inflation low-FEDFUNDS trigger has a positive average 20-day S&P 500 return or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power versus CPIAUCSL alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates improving operating leverage and goods-sector demand; positive 6-month momentum in INDPRO predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value was 98.9462 for 2013-07-01 available by the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if positive 6-month INDPRO momentum is not associated with higher-than-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A downside break in housing starts signals weaker interest-sensitive demand and lower future construction-linked earnings; when HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average by more than 10%, S&P 500 returns are negative over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 6-month moving average by > 10%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value was 836 for 2013-06-01 available by the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside-break triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or if average triggered returns exceed the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth accompanied by falling unemployment confirms broad labor-market improvement rather than labor-force distortion; when PAYEMS rises strongly and UNRATE falls, S&P 500 returns are positive over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average gain > 150 thousand per month and UNRATE falls >= 0.2pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value was 136038 for 2013-07-01 and UNRATE vintage value was 7.4
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal has no higher 20-day return than either single-series signal or if triggered observations produce positive returns less often than the unconditional base rate.

mechanism_narrative: A rising FEDFUNDS path tightens financial conditions and increases discount rates; when FEDFUNDS crosses above its 12-month average by at least 0.25 percentage points, S&P 500 forward returns are negative over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its 12-month average by >= 0.25pp
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value was 0.09 for 2013-07-01 available by the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS tightening triggers have positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if drawdown frequency after triggers is not greater than the unconditional drawdown frequency.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-rate expansion, positive real GDP growth confirms durable aggregate demand while cheap funding supports equity multiples; rising GDPC1 with FEDFUNDS below 0.5% predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage value was 15648.7 for 2013-04-01 and FEDFUNDS vintage value was 0.09
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1-growth and low-FEDFUNDS regime fails to produce a positive 5-day average S&P 500 return or if its hit rate is below the unconditional positive-return rate.
